channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: At least 24 killed in Pakistan train blast claimed by separatist militants
item.description: A bomb blast has hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the latest major attack claimed by separatist militants.

media:group.media:description: The attack is the latest in a series of strikes on trains and infrastructure in the province.

item.title: Perth's Cottesloe revamp to go ahead but more funding needed, says mayor
item.description: An oceanfront redevelopment at Perth's iconic Cottesloe Beach will go ahead with $15 million in funding, but the mayor says more financial assistance is needed from the state government to complete the project.
item.dc:creator: Yiying Li

media:group.media:description: The existing car park on Cottesloe Beach will be repurposed.

item.title: Trump skips son's wedding due to his 'love of the United States'
item.description: US President Donald Trump misses his son's wedding ceremony in the Bahamas, citing his "love of the United States" as a key reason to stay in Washington.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump Jr and Bettina Anderson plan to hold a White House celebration when the president can attend.

item.title: Politics and faith collide over choice for Tibet's next Dalai Lama
item.description: Tibetan Buddhists believe the Dalai Lama decides when and where he will reincarnate. But China's ruling Communist Party says it must choose who will replace him.

media:group.media:description: As Tibetans in Australia make their case for the Dalai Lama to choose his successor — as has happened for centuries — there is a real fear the Chinese government will install its own choice.

item.title: Man charged after 14yo restaurant worker threatened with knife
item.description: Police say a 30-year-old man who allegedly demanded money from a 14-year-old worker at knifepoint, on the NSW Central Coast, was tackled to the ground by two other men.
item.dc:creator: Mary-Louise Vince

media:group.media:description: A man allegedly attempted to rob the Long Jetty Chinese Restaurant on Saturday night at knifepoint.

item.title: North Queensland crowd raises record figure for Arrow and MND charity
item.description: North Queensland climbs into the NRL's top six with an impressive 30-18 victory over South Sydney in Townsville, where fans raise thousands of dollars for the Fight MND charity.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Jai Arrow revealed this week that he was diagnosed with MND late last year.

item.title: Air and land search continues for missing 31-year-old man in Arnhem Land
item.description: The NT Police Force says it continues to hold concerns for the welfare of the man, who was last seen early on Friday.

media:group.media:description: Marius was last seen early Friday.

item.title: More than 20 people feared trapped after Philippines building collapse
item.description: Search and rescue operations are underway in the Philippines after a nine-storey building collapsed.

media:group.media:description: Workers survey the collapsed building in Angeles City.

item.title: Man dies after shark attack in Far North Queensland
item.description: A 39-year-old man has died following a shark attack in Far North Queensland.

media:group.media:description: Hull Heads is a seaside town about 160 kilometres south of Cairns in Queensland's far north.

item.title: Russian missile strike kills four people in Kyiv
item.description: Russia uses a hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile to hit Kyiv, killing four people and injuring at least 20 others.

media:group.media:description: Hundreds of people take cover inside a local metro station in an attempt to stay safe during the missile strike in Kyiv, Ukraine.

item.title: Drink driver crashes into pole, over embankment but escapes uninjured, police say
item.description: SA Police say a woman has lost her licence after crashing into a timber pole and onto a beach in Adelaide while drink driving.

media:group.media:description: A driver has been issued with an immediate loss of licence after crashing into a post and travelling off the embankment at Aldinga Beach in South Australia.

item.title: Police officer injured during stolen vehicle crash in North Queensland
item.description: The officer was taken to Mackay Hospital in a stable condition with lower leg injury.

media:group.media:description: A cordon zone has been declared in Paradise Street, Paget.

item.title: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic
item.description: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Bulldogs get home in a see-sawing contest against the Demons.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Pollard

media:group.media:description: Toby Greene celebrates one of 14 goals GWS kicked in the third quarter against Brisbane.

item.title: My dog Teddy was more than just a pet. We needed each other
item.description: Judith Geppert has an impressive resume of achievements and celebrations, but her capabilities are often questioned. Her dog Teddy, however, never treated her any differently.

media:group.media:description: Judith says giving Teddy a good home enriched her life.

item.title: Sexual health provider 'crippled by overheads' shuts Melbourne clinic
item.description: The service says government funding has failed to keep pace with increased demand and overhead costs, with rates of sexually transmitted diseases at near-record levels.
item.dc:creator: Costa Haritos

media:group.media:description: Rising costs have been blamed for the closure of a Melbourne sexual health service.

item.title: Pocock open to party of independents as One Nation support climbs
item.description: The independent senator says Australians are frustrated with a political system that "does not work for them".
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Independent Senator David Pocock has left the door open to forming a party with other independents. (ABC News: Matt Roberts)

item.title: Recent fall in auction clearance rates could be good news for first home buyers
item.description: Negative sentiment around the property sector has started to make its way into the marketplace since the federal budget, but real estate experts say it may prove to be good news for first-home buyers.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Auction clearance rates have decreased around the country following changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax.

item.title: Exhibition sheds fresh light on WA's pre-colonial history
item.description: Oral histories from Menang Noongar elders are combined with AI renderings of life before colonisation in an exhibition at the WA State Library.
item.dc:creator: Will Corbett and Samantha Goerling
item.category: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

media:group.media:description: Menang elder Carol Pettersen maintains a strong connection to country.

item.title: Heavyweight champ avoids shock upset with controversial stoppage
item.description: Ukraine's unbeaten heavyweight world champion Oleksandr Usyk stopped Dutch former kickboxer Rico Verhoeven with one second left in the penultimate round of their WBC title fight.

media:group.media:description: Oleksandr Usyk (left) has retained the WBC World Heavyweight Championship.

item.title: Two cars set alight, house damaged in arson attack in Melbourne's west
item.description: The fire spread from the vehicles to a townhouse in Braybrook in the early hours of this morning.

media:group.media:description: Two cars were set alight with one fire spreading to a home in Royal Road, Braybrook.

item.title: Export name change coming for Australian prosecco
item.description: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.
item.dc:creator: Annie Brown

media:group.media:description: Almost half of the prosecco produced in Australia comes from Victoria's King Valley.

item.title: Man, 20, charged over alleged rape of elderly woman in own NT home
item.description: The man was arrested two days after allegedly fleeing the scene in Woodroffe, outside Darwin.

media:group.media:description: The man was arrested by police on Friday night.

item.title: ABC wins 11 categories at Tasmanian Media Awards
item.description: The ABC has won 11 of the 20 categories at this year's Media Entertainment & Arts Alliance (MEAA) Tasmanian Media Awards, while WIN News reporter Brent Costelloe took home the prestigious journalist of the year award.

media:group.media:description: Back row (L-R): Adam Morton, David Killick, Nina Hendy, Ebony Abblitt, Chris Rowbottom, Olivia Hicks, Fiona Blackwood, Adam Holmes. Front row (L-R): Morgan Timms, Louise Miolin, Brent Costelloe, Sophie Jaggers, Ebony ten Broeke, Madeleine Rojahn.

item.title: Endangered turtle found to have survived record-breaking flood
item.description: Experts are relieved to see the number of endangered Manning River turtles holding steady, a year after devastating flooding in northern New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: Marina Trajkovich and Emma Siossian

media:group.media:description: A Manning River turtle that was released back to the wild, after being rescued during bushfires in 2020.

item.title: Data reveals V/Line punctuality on two routes dropped to lowest in almost a decade
item.description: The latest V/Line data reveals only 56 per cent of services arrived on time on two regional train routes during Victoria's first month of free public transport.
item.dc:creator: Nethma Dandeniya and Philippe Perez

media:group.media:description: V/Line's punctuality was lower on the majority of regional routes across Victoria's first month of free public transport, as compared to March 2026.
